source_intake_record.raw_question_text: If $\triangle L M N \sim \triangle Q R S, Q R=35, R S=37, S Q=12$, and $N L=5$, find the perimeter of $\triangle L M N$.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly states the similarity relation and all needed side lengths: QR=35, RS=37, SQ=12, NL=5
the perimeter can be derived from the textual correspondence in triangle LMN ~ triangle QRS without any explicit visual attribute from the image
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false suggest an image may exist in the source format, but the provided text appears sufficient for solving
multi_step: solving requires identifying corresponding sides from triangle LMN ~ triangle QRS, specifically NL ↔ SQ
then a scale factor must be computed from NL=5 and SQ=12
then the perimeter of triangle QRS must be summed and scaled to obtain the perimeter of triangle LMN
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single clear value: 35
the problem has a deterministic geometry computation path based on stated lengths and similarity
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true support reliable downstream checking

decision_reasons: question, answer, and image assets are all present with no registry issues
high verifiability score and numeric answer support reliable checking
text appears sufficient to recover the core geometry semantics even if source formatting referenced an image
multi-step reasoning value is solid and there is no evidence of missing core semantic support
